data.purpose: Defines coding session workflow. General — not project-bound. Load explicitly at the start of any implementation session.

context.user_background: C# and Java. Currently transitioning to Python.
context.condition: This background is the condition under which the session structure below applies. Reassess if language or familiarity level changes significantly.

session_structure.condition: Applies until user is trained enough to begin modifying or contributing code, at which point workflow is explicitly reassessed.

session_structure.rule: Walk through code together line by line
session_structure.condition: Applies in early familiarity phase. Either party flags anything unclear, wrong, or assumption-laden. Stop and resolve before moving on.

session_structure.rule: As familiarity grows, shift to targeted review of non-obvious parts
session_structure.condition: Transition point is not fixed — flagged when line-by-line is producing diminishing returns.

conceptual_mistakes.definition: Two types: (1) misalignment between what user intends and what Claude produces, (2) blindspots in Claude's own code — assumptions not surfaced, patterns carried over incorrectly from other languages.
conceptual_mistakes.handling: Both caught during walkthrough. Claude names its own mistakes directly when found. No softening.

notes: Migrated from workflow.txt uploaded session 9, 2026-04-30. Communication rules and memory loading not duplicated here — governed by meta.behaviour_personality and user preferences respectively.
